text: prevent model using data set column predictor, assign new role, preserved persistent homology step ignored vectorization step (outputs new predictor columns). simplicity, choose common model ML classification, penalized logistic regression. fix mixture coefficient use LASSO rather ridge regression prepare penalty parameter tuning. generate complete hyperparameter tuning grid crossing grids generated two unspecified parameters:
code: # specify a pre-processing recipe scale_seq <- seq(0, 3, by = .05) recipe(embedding ~ sample, data = klein_train) |>    update_role(sample, new_role = "data set") |>    step_pd_point_cloud(sample, max_hom_degree = tune("vr_degree")) |>    step_vpd_euler_characteristic_curve(sample, xseq = scale_seq) |>    print() -> klein_rec #>  #> ── Recipe ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Inputs #> Number of variables by role #> outcome:  1 #> data set: 1 #>  #> ── Operations #> • persistent features from a Rips filtration of: sample #> • Euler characteristic curve of: sample # specify a classification model logistic_reg(penalty = tune(), mixture = 1) |>    set_mode("classification") |>    set_engine("glmnet") |>    print() -> klein_lm #> Logistic Regression Model Specification (classification) #>  #> Main Arguments: #>   penalty = tune() #>   mixture = 1 #>  #> Computational engine: glmnet # generate a hyperparameter tuning grid klein_rec_grid <- grid_regular(   extract_parameter_set_dials(klein_rec), levels = 3,   filter = c(vr_degree > 0) ) klein_lm_grid <- grid_regular(   extract_parameter_set_dials(klein_lm), levels = 5 ) klein_grid <- merge(klein_rec_grid, klein_lm_grid)

title: A recipes Extension for Persistent Homology and Its Vectorizations
text: evaluate model across hyperparameter grid using cross-validation, using area sensitivity–specificity (ROC) curve: results, obtain best-performing parameter setting:
code: # optimize the model performance klein_res <- tune_grid(   klein_lm,   preprocessor = klein_rec,   resamples = klein_folds,   grid = klein_grid,   metrics = metric_set(roc_auc) ) klein_res |>    select_best(metric = "roc_auc") |>    print() -> klein_best #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>        penalty vr_degree .config              #>          <dbl>     <int> <chr>                #> 1 0.0000000001         1 Preprocessor1_Model1

title: A recipes Extension for Persistent Homology and Its Vectorizations
text: optimal setting includes VR homology degree GLM penalty, pre-processing recipe predictive model must finalized order fit final model full training set: Finally, evaluate fitted model testing set:
code: klein_rec_fin <- klein_rec |> finalize_recipe(klein_best) |> prep() klein_lm_fin <- klein_lm |> finalize_model(klein_best) klein_fit <- fit(   klein_lm_fin,   formula(klein_rec_fin),   data = bake(klein_rec_fin, new_data = klein_train) ) klein_fit |>    predict(     new_data = bake(klein_rec_fin, new_data = klein_test),     type = "prob"   ) |>    bind_cols(select(klein_test, embedding)) |>    roc_auc(truth = embedding, .pred_flat) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   .metric .estimator .estimate #>   <chr>   <chr>          <dbl> #> 1 roc_auc binary          0.92

code: # toy data set klein_sampler <- function(n, prob = .5) {   if (rbinom(1, 1, prob) == 0) {     tdaunif::sample_klein_flat(n)   } else {     tdaunif::sample_klein_tube(n)   } } sample_data <- data.frame(   id = LETTERS[seq(4L)],   sample = I(c(replicate(4L, klein_sampler(60), simplify = FALSE))) )  # options to calibrate homological degree hom_degree(range = c(2, 5)) #> Homological Degree (quantitative) #> Range: [2, 5] hom_degree() %>% get_hom_range(x = sample_data[, 2, drop = FALSE]) #> Homological Degree (quantitative) #> Range: [0, 1] hom_degree() %>% get_hom_range(x = sample_data[, 2, drop = FALSE], max_dim = 5) #> Homological Degree (quantitative) #> Range: [0, 3]  # heterogeneous data types hetero_data <- tibble(dataset = list(mtcars, nhtemp, eurodist, HairEyeColor)) hetero_data %>%    mutate(class = vapply(dataset, function(x) class(x)[[1L]], "")) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   dataset             class      #>   <list>              <chr>      #> 1 <df [32 × 11]>      data.frame #> 2 <ts [60]>           ts         #> 3 <dist [210]>        dist       #> 4 <table [4 × 4 × 2]> table      get_hom_range(   hom_degree(),   hetero_data,   max_dim = 60 ) #> Homological Degree (quantitative) #> Range: [0, 20]
